vulnerability.name: CVE-2024-0056
vulnerability.description: Microsoft.Data.SqlClient TLS bypass — adversary-in-the-middle attack possible against SQL Server connections

statements.impact_statement: HIPAA CRITICAL: The healthcare platform connects to Azure SQL Server containing PHI (patient records, diagnoses, prescriptions) via Microsoft.Data.SqlClient 5.1.2. An attacker on the same Azure VNet can perform an AiTM attack against the TLS channel, intercepting SQL queries and responses containing unencrypted PHI. Azure VNet isolation provides partial mitigation but internal network threats are in scope for HIPAA.
statements.action_statement: Upgrade Microsoft.Data.SqlClient to 5.1.3+. Verify all connection strings explicitly set Encrypt=True;TrustServerCertificate=False. Implement network-level controls (Azure Firewall, NSG) to restrict SQL traffic to authorized service identities only.

vulnerability.name: CVE-2024-21319
vulnerability.description: Microsoft Identity Web JWT/OIDC denial of service via malformed OpenID Connect metadata

statements.impact_statement: HIPAA Availability: The patient portal uses OpenID Connect with Azure AD B2C. An attacker can send a crafted OIDC metadata document causing memory exhaustion in the authentication middleware, effectively denying patient access to their health records. HIPAA §164.312(a)(2)(ii) requires emergency access procedure when primary auth is unavailable.
statements.action_statement: Upgrade Microsoft.AspNetCore.Authentication.OpenIdConnect to 8.0.1+. Implement circuit breaker pattern for OIDC metadata refresh. Configure backup authentication path for emergency clinical access per HIPAA §164.312(a)(2)(ii) Emergency Access Procedure.

vulnerability.name: CVE-2023-36049
vulnerability.description: .NET FTP client CRLF injection / elevation of privilege

statements.impact_statement: The healthcare platform does not use the .NET FTP client. All external integrations use HTTPS/REST or HL7 MLLP. The FTP client vulnerability in .NET is present in the runtime but the vulnerable code path is never exercised. Azure App Service does not expose FTP client functionality.
statements.action_statement: No action required for this specific vulnerability. Apply November 2023 .NET 8 cumulative update as part of regular patching to address other potential issues in the same patch bundle.

vulnerability.name: CVE-2024-38095
vulnerability.description: .NET System.Formats.Asn1 infinite loop DoS via malformed ASN.1

statements.impact_statement: The FHIR server processes X.509 certificates in the Smart-on-FHIR authorization flow. A malformed client certificate in the EHR-to-EHR trust chain could trigger the ASN.1 infinite loop, causing CPU exhaustion on the FHIR API server. In a clinical setting, this could disrupt real-time lab result delivery or e-prescribing workflows.
statements.action_statement: Upgrade to .NET 8.0.7 (July 2024 cumulative update). Add certificate size and format validation at the API gateway (Azure API Management) before passing to the FHIR server to reject malformed certificates early.

vulnerability.name: CVE-2024-21401
vulnerability.description: Microsoft Entra SAML assertion privilege escalation

statements.impact_statement: Version 2.15.3 is above the reported fix version 2.15.1. However, the SAML integration with Azure AD B2C custom policies may reintroduce the vulnerability if third-party SAML assertions are trusted. Currently assessing whether the custom B2C policy for hospital EHR federated login is vulnerable to the assertion crafting attack.
statements.action_statement: Audit Azure AD B2C custom SAML policies for attribute mapping rules. Ensure hospital federated login IdP metadata is pinned to trusted certificates. Verify Microsoft.Identity.Web 2.15.3 includes the GHSA patch. Assessment due 2026-03-09.

vulnerability.name: CVE-2024-20656
vulnerability.description: Visual Studio Developer Command Prompt local privilege escalation

statements.impact_statement: Visual Studio is not installed on any production healthcare SaaS servers. This vulnerability affects developer workstation tooling only. The ASP.NET Core runtime used in production does not include VS tooling components. Azure App Service and AKS production containers do not have Visual Studio.
statements.action_statement: Ensure developer workstations running Visual Studio are patched to 17.8.4+. Production systems are not affected.
